# Repository Service for TUF Command Line Interface

{{ OpenSSF Scorecard }} {{ Tests and Lint }} {{ Coverage }}

The Repository Service for TUF Command Line Interface (CLI) is a CLI Python
application to manage the Repository Service for TUF.

The CLI supports the initial setup, termed a ceremony, where the first
repository metadata are signed and the service is configured, update metadata,
sign metadata, etc.

This CLI is part of the Repository Service for TUF (RSTUF).

## Installation

Using pip:

```shell
$ pip install repository-service-tuf
```

## Usage

Please, check the [Repository Service for TUF Guide](https://repository-service-tuf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide/repository-service-tuf-cli/index.html)
for more details.

## Contributing

Please, visit the [Repository Service for TUF Development Guide](https://repository-service-tuf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/devel/index.html#development-guide).

Check our [CONTRIBUTING.rst](https://github.com/repository-service-tuf/repository-service-tuf-cli/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.rst)
for more details on how to contribute to this repository.

## License

[MIT](https://github.com/repository-service-tuf/repository-service-tuf-cli/blob/main/LICENSE)

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.metadata.update.update
documentation:
Perform metadata update and send result to API.

* If `--metadata-url TEXT` is passed, the latest root will be fetched from
metadata storage.

* If `--in FILENAME` is passed, input is not read from API but from local
FILENAME.

* If both `--metadata-url TEXT` and `--in FILENAME` are passed, then
`--metadata-url TEXT` will have higher priority.

* If `--out [FILENAME]` is passed, result is written to local FILENAME
(in addition to being sent to API).

* If `--dry-run` is passed, result is not sent to API.
You can still pass `--out [FILENAME]` to store the result locally.

* If `--in` and `--dry-run` are passed, `--api-server` admin option and
`SERVER` from config will be ignored.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.metadata.sign.sign
documentation:
Perform sign for pending event and send result to API.

* If `--in FILENAME` is passed, input is not read from API but from local
FILENAME.

* If `--out [FILENAME]` is passed, result is written to local FILENAME
(in addition to being sent to API).

* If `--dry-run` is passed, result is not sent to API.
You can still pass `--out [FILENAME]` to store the result locally.

* If `--in` and `--dry-run` are passed, `--api-server` admin option and
`SERVER` from config will be ignored.

documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023-2024 Repository Service for TUF Contributors

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.ceremony.ceremony
documentation:
Perform ceremony and send result to API to trigger bootstrap.

 
* If `--out [FILENAME]` is passed, result is written to local FILENAME
(in addition to being sent to API).

 
* If `--dry-run` is passed, result is not sent to API.
You can still pass `--out [FILENAME]` to store the result locally.
The `--api-server` admin option and `SERVER` from config will be ignored.

Note: 'BOOTSTRAP_PAYLOAD' argument must be generated by using:

'rstuf admin ceremony' command.

Note: 'METADATA_UPDATE_PAYLOAD' argument must be generated by using:

documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023-2024 Repository Service for TUF Contributors

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.import_artifacts.import_artifacts
documentation:
Import artifacts information from exported CSV file and send it to RSTUF
API deployment.

 
Note: there are two additional requirements for this command:
 
1) sqlalchemy needs to be installed in order to use this command:
pip install repository-service-tuf[sqlalchemy,psycopg2]

 
2) '--api-server' admin option or 'SERVER' in RSTUF config set

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.artifact.add.add
documentation:
Add artifacts to the TUF metadata.

A POST /api/v1/artifacts/ request to the RSTUF API service is carried out,
where:
- file info is discovered and added to the request payload. The blake2b-256
cryptographic hash function is used to hash the file.
- `custom` key of the payload is an empty object
- `path` key of the payload is defined by the user

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.artifact.delete.delete
documentation:
Delete artifacts to the TUF metadata.

A POST /api/v1/artifacts/delete request to the RSTUF API service
is carried out.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.task.info.info
documentation:
Retrieve task state.

A GET /api/v1/task/ request to the RSTUF API service is carried out.

Prompt for days until expiry for role, returns days and expiry date.

Use per-role defaults from ExpirationSettings.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._configure_root_keys_prompt
documentation:
Prompt dialog to add or remove root key in passed root, until user exit.

- Print if and how many root keys are missing to meet the threshold
- Print current root keys
- Prompt for user choice to add or remove key, or to skip (exit)
    - "continue" choice is only available, if threshold is met
    - "remove" choice is only available, if keys exist
    - "add" choice is only shown, if "remove" or "continue" is available,
      otherwise, we branch right into "add" dialog

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._configure_online_key_prompt
documentation: Prompt dialog to set or optionally update the online key.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._add_signature_prompt
documentation: Prompt for signing key and add signature to metadata until success.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._path_prompt
documentation: Prompt for path name until success.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._filter_root_verification_results
documentation: Filter unverified results with distinct relevant fields.

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._print_keys_for_signing
documentation:
Print public keys eligible for signing and return in printed order.

The indexed output can be used to choose a signing key (1-based).

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.cli.admin.helpers._print_root_keys
documentation:
Print current root keys and return in printed order.

The indexed output can be used to choose a key (1-based).

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.helpers.cli.create_artifact_add_payload_from_filepath
documentation:
Create the payload for the API request of `POST api/v1/artifacts/`.
The blake2b-256 cryptographic hash function is used to hash the file.

```
param filepath

The file path to calculate the hash.

param path

The path defined in the metadata for the artifact.
```

functions.name: repository_service_tuf.helpers.api_client.send_payload
documentation:
Send 'payload' to a given 'settings.SERVER'.

Args:
    settings: the command context settings object
    url: one of the URLs to a given endpoint as defined in api_client.py
    payload: dictionary containing the payload to send
    expected_msg: expected message to receive as a response to the request
    command_name: name of the command sending the payload, used for logging
    expected_status_code: [Optional] expected status code. Default: 202
Returns:
    Task id of the job sending the payload.
